thesis: A sampled drone frame, gate camera clip, and operator note indicate a solvent container breach near a blocked service lane. VesperGrid recommends candidate containment routes while preserving source lineage and uncertainty.

evidence.summary: Bright thermal band appears beside stacked polymer containers in Sector 4.
Service vehicles are queuing at the same lane marked clear in the radio note.
Operator reports hydrant pressure instability and asks for tanker relay confirmation.
Sustained 21 km/h wind is pushing vapor toward fuel-adjacent storage lanes.

zones.label: Leak Core
zones.rationale: Thermal keyframe and operator note agree on active containment risk.

zones.label: Vapor Drift
zones.rationale: Wind strip projects vapor toward fuel-adjacent storage lane.

zones.label: Blocked Service Lane
zones.rationale: Video sample contradicts the radio claim that the route is clear.

actions.title: Route containment team through Junction E instead of Gate 4
actions.caveat: Candidate plan only: verify obstruction at Gate 4 before dispatch.

actions.title: Stage foam unit at north-east perimeter
actions.caveat: Hydrant pressure is unstable; tanker relay remains required.

actions.title: Hold fuel-adjacent lane access until vapor drift is rechecked
actions.caveat: Wind sensitivity is high; rerun after next sensor update.

uncertainties.title: Route contradiction
uncertainties.detail: Radio note says Gate 4 is clear, but CCTV sample shows a vehicle queue.

uncertainties.title: Wind-driven vapor drift
uncertainties.detail: Small wind changes alter the recommended perimeter by 60-90 meters.

gpu.label: MI300X · KV cache
gpu.workload: Paged attention reserved for warm Qwen-VL context

gpu.label: MI300X · headroom
gpu.workload: Free VRAM available for larger model (e.g. Qwen2.5-VL-32B)

brief: Highest-confidence risk: solvent leak core with vapor drift toward fuel-adjacent lane.
Candidate plan: route containment through Junction E because Gate 4 has conflicting evidence.
Resource constraint: hydrant pressure instability requires tanker relay confirmation.
Human approval required before dispatch; VesperGrid is decision support, not an autonomous authority.
